mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding output capacity and rising corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for INDPRO trend signal falls below 0.02 or the signal shows no predictive power over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, monetary policy becomes restrictive, raising the discount rate on future cash flows and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals stagflationary pressures that erode corporate profitability and investor confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods of rising UNRATE and elevated FEDFUNDS, 5-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the signal's win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (positive GDP growth), a month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals robust residential investment and consumer confidence, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During expansions, rising HOUST does not predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window) or the proportion of positive returns does not exceed 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a monthly decline in RSAFS, 5-day SPX returns are negative less than 55% of the time, or the average return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines concurrently with a rise in retail sales (RSAFS), the economy experiences a demand-supply mismatch leading to inventory depletion and price pressure, which negatively impacts corporate margins and S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline and RSAFS month-over-month rise
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling INDPRO and rising RSAFS does not produce negative 20-day returns more frequently than 55% of the time, or the signal fails a binomial test (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle (federal funds rate declining by at least 25 basis points over the prior 3 months), rising industrial production validates the accommodative policy and amplifies positive equity returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS has declined 25bp over 3 months, rising INDPRO does not lead to positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window, or the win rate of the signal does not exceed 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL), erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase interest rates, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The level of CPIAUCSL does not produce a consistent negative rank IC below -0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or the signal shows no significant predictive power in a linear regression (t-stat > -1.65).
